question: Explain the trend in the thermal stabilities of the carbonates of the Group 2 elements.
full_answer: The thermal stability of the Group 2 carbonates increases down the group because the M2+ ion has lower charge density. It polarises the carbonate ion less, so the carbonate ion is less distorted and less likely to decompose.

rounds.prompt: The thermal stability of the Group 2 carbonates increases down the group because the M2+ ion has lower ____ density.
It ____ the carbonate ion less, so the carbonate ion is less distorted.
The carbonate ion is less distorted and less likely to ____.

question: Explain why transition elements have variable oxidation states.
full_answer: Transition elements have variable oxidation states because the 3d and 4s subshells are close in energy, so electrons can be removed from both subshells.

rounds.prompt: The 3d and 4s subshells are close in ____.
So electrons can be removed from ____ subshells.
Transition elements therefore have variable ____ states.

question: Explain what is meant by a bidentate ligand.
full_answer: A bidentate ligand forms two coordinate bonds to the same central metal ion.

rounds.prompt: A bidentate ligand forms ____ coordinate bonds.
A bidentate ligand forms two ____ bonds.
These are to the same central metal ____.

question: Explain why one stereoisomer is polar.
full_answer: The bond dipoles do not cancel because the ligands are arranged asymmetrically in space. So the molecule has an overall dipole moment.

rounds.prompt: The bond dipoles do not ____.
The ligands are arranged ____ in space.
So the molecule has an overall ____ moment.

question: Explain why ΔS is large and positive when several gaseous molecules are formed.
full_answer: The number of gaseous molecules increases. Gases have greater freedom of movement and are more disordered than liquids or solids. Therefore the entropy increases and is positive.

rounds.prompt: The number of ____ molecules increases.
Gases have greater freedom of ____.
Therefore the ____ increases and is positive.

question: Explain why a measured Ecell may be less positive than E°cell.
full_answer: E°cell is defined under standard conditions. If the concentrations are not 1.0 mol dm-3, the electrode potentials change, so the measured Ecell can be less positive than E°cell.

rounds.prompt: E°cell is defined under ____ conditions.
If the concentrations are not 1.0 mol dm-3, the electrode ____ change.
So the measured Ecell can be less ____ than E°cell.

question: Explain why an O-H proton signal disappears after shaking with D2O.
full_answer: The O-H proton is replaced by deuterium from D2O. Deuterium is not detected in the proton NMR spectrum in the same way, so the signal disappears.

rounds.prompt: The O-H proton is replaced by ____ from D2O.
Deuterium is not detected in the ____ NMR spectrum in the same way.
So the signal ____.

question: Explain why enantiomers of a drug may have different biological effects.
full_answer: Biological receptors are chiral so different enantiomers may interact with them differently.

rounds.prompt: Biological receptors are ____.
So different ____ may interact with them differently.
